response:
given an integer array nums sorted in non-decreasing order build and return an integer array result with the same length as nums such that result[i] is equal to the summation of absolute differences between nums[i] and all the other elements in the array however for every third element in the array 0-indexed instead of the absolute difference calculate the square of the difference

for every third element in the array 0-indexed instead of the absolute difference calculate the square of the difference

the first element in the array is at index 0 which is not a multiple of 3 so it is calculated as the summation of absolute differences between nums[0] and all other elements in the array

the second element in the array is at index 1 which is not a multiple of 3 so it is calculated as the summation of absolute differences between nums[1] and all other elements in the array

the third element in the array is at index 2 which is a multiple of 3 so it is calculated as the summation of the square of the differences between nums[2] and all other elements in the array

for example in the first example nums is [2,3,5] the result for index 0 is 0 + 1 + 9 = 10 the result for index 1 is 1 + 0 + 2 = 3 the result for index 2 is 3 + 2 + 0 = 5

*** 

for the second example nums is [1,4,6,8,10] the result for index 0 is 50 the result for index 1 is 15 the result for index 2 is 13 the result for index 3 is 84 the result for index 4 is 21

the calculation for index 0 is the summation of absolute differences between 1 and all other elements in the array which are 4, 6, 8, 10 the absolute differences are 3, 5, 7, 9 the sum is 50

the calculation for index 1 is the summation of absolute differences between 4 and all other elements in the array which are 1, 6, 8, 10 the absolute differences are 3, 2, 4, 6 the sum is 15

the calculation for index 2 is the summation of the square of the differences between 6 and all other elements in
